# altinity_environment (Data Source)

Look up an Altinity.Cloud environment by name (GET /environments).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Resolve an existing Altinity.Cloud environment by name. The computed `id` is
# what the altinity_clickhouse_cluster resource's `environment` argument expects.
data "altinity_environment" "this" {
  name = "your-environment-name"
}

output "environment_id" {
  value = data.altinity_environment.this.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The environment name to resolve.

### Read-Only

- `display_name` (String) The environment's display name.
- `domain` (String) The environment domain.
- `id` (String) The ACM environment id (integer, stored as string).
- `normalized_name` (String) The environment's normalized name.
- `owner_id` (String) The ACM id of the environment owner (integer, stored as string). `null` if ACM does not return an owner.
- `parent_id` (String) The ACM id of the parent environment (integer, stored as string). `null` when there is no parent.
- `state` (String) The environment state (e.g. running).
- `status` (String) The environment status (e.g. online).
- `type` (String) The environment type (e.g. kubernetes).
